entry.title: South China Morning Post: 18 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 18 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-18 23:15PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: outbreaks at Hong Kong public hospitals grow, as John Lee warns quarter of healthcare resources could be used up by end of the month

  • Hongkongers send 400 million disposable coffee cups to landfills every year, according to green group’s estimates

  • Tesla’s Chinese battery maker CATL is scoping out factory sites in Mexico close to the Texas border
  • How many aircraft carriers does China need? One analyst says at least 6
  • Asian foreign-currency bond issuance falls to four-year low amid Chinese developers’ woes, US rate rises
  • Malaysia seizes US$18 million in elephant tusks, rhino horns and pangolin scales, possibly destined for China
  • Yellen says US allies must not allow China to dominate raw materials, technologies, and thwart ‘unfair trade practices’
  • Japan won’t cap defence spending amid increased ‘anxiety’ over Russia, China, North Korea
  • Pacific island nations call for World Court to rule on big polluters’ obligations
  • Coronavirus: North Korea claims it will soon ‘defuse the crisis completely’ as Asian neighbours fight Covid resurgence
  • Thailand protests: Israeli spyware used to target activists and lawyers, report finds
  • Inflation in New Zealand hits 32-year high as fuel, food, housing costs surge
  • South Korea’s Yoon faces becoming a ‘lame duck’ as nepotism claims, wife’s gaffes hit ratings
  • Which cities are the most expensive for expats to live in?
  • Rishi Sunak widens lead in race to become next UK prime minister
  • Biden’s Medal of Freedom picks a reminder of US lack of regard for Asian-Americans
  • Top US Covid-19 adviser Anthony Fauci expects to retire by end of Joe Biden’s current term
  • UK Royal Air Force halts flights at airbase after runway melts in heatwave
  • Ukraine war: Russian shelling kills 6 in Donbas; Vladimir Putin and Türkiye’s Erdogan to discuss grain exports

  • China’s economic recovery depends on policy support and a viable Covid-19 exit strategy
  • Hong Kong stocks surge as developers pace rebound after China moves to ease funding crunch while Meituan, Alibaba lead tech rebound
  • China electric-car makers BYD, Xpeng, Li Auto, Nio stand to benefit as lower loan rates recharge sales after Covid-19 woes
  • Indifference to Hong Kong’s extraordinary wealth divide evident in civil service pay dispute
  • China instructs banks to open the credit taps for developers to finish building their homes, and avert mortgage borrowers’ revolt
  • How beating stage 4 lung cancer inspired a hotel manager to become a wellness tourism pioneer
  • Netflix K-drama review: Remarriage & Desires – matchmaking melodrama starring Kim Hee-seon and Lee Hyun-wook is slick enough, but lacks fireworks
  • 5 ancient Chinese instruments come alive in rousing Hong Kong concert inspired by China’s Dunhuang cave paintings
  • Cartier’s latest high jewellery collection combines creativity and timeless designs, says its style and heritage director Pierre Rainero
  • Baroque paintings, music and animation take art lovers in Hong Kong on an immersive journey
  • YSL spring/summer 2023 show in Moroccan desert is reminiscent of a David Bowie science fiction film
  • Weird tourist attraction in Western Australia draws visitors to rural town’s annual Bush Races
  • China’s political system wasn’t always highly centralised – once it was almost a federal nation like the United States
  • Who were Ivana Trump’s 4 ex-husbands? She and Donald had kids Ivanka, Donald Jr. and Eric before he cheated with Marla Maples, but she also had a ‘Cold War marriage’ and a long-term ‘boy toy’ …
  • Elon Musk’s 6 biggest moneymakers, ranked: the billionaire may be in trouble with Twitter, but he’s got successful companies SpaceX, Tesla, Neuralink and The Boring Company bringing in over a trillion
  • Who was Elvis Presley’s only grandson, Benjamin Keough? Riley Keough’s brother, once the stepson of Michael Jackson while married to Lisa Marie, took his own life after struggling with addiction
  • Is Novak Djokovic the most eccentric man in tennis? He ate grass off the Wimbledon court after beating Nick Kyrgios, refuses to get his Covid-19 vaccination, and adores his poodle Pierre
  • Inside Chris Hemsworth’s superhero-worthy watch collection: the Thor actor dons Rolex, Bulgari, Audemars Piguet, and has two Tag Heuer timepieces – one seen in Netflix’s Spiderhead
  • Missing Princess Mako: what does Japan’s royal family think of her move to New York? Kako, Aiko and father Fumihito support her, but Japan’s PM Fumio Kishida is still not ready for a female emperor
  • Meet Paridhi Shroff, Gautam Adani’s daughter-in-law: she’s married to Karan, the son of Asia’s richest man, and had Mukesh Ambani and Narendra Modi at her 2013 wedding
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 19 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • All work and no play as scorching heat takes toll on kids’ activities
  • Fukui company’s barley straws fill a niche during plastic-free push
  • 12,696 COVID-19 cases logged in Tokyo, doubling from week ago
  • Kyoto Animation marks 3rd year of deadly arson attack at studio
  • Suspect willing to die to ‘liberate’ members of religious group
  • Letter touches on plan to kill Abe, who ‘wields the most influence’
  • Workers in Japan return to school to boost skills, motivation
  • Edison’s letter of thanks to Shiga inventor found after a century
  • Confectionery in Japan creates hard candy that tastes like wine
  • Yellen calls out China trade practices in S. Korea visit
  • S.Korea's foreign minister flies to Japan for talks on historical disputes
  • N.Korea says it is nearing end of COVID crisis as Asian neighbours fight resurgence
  • Another state of emergency declared in Sri Lanka as acting president takes reins
  • Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/ Iya Valley: Swinging vines and bridges bring Tarzan and Jane to mind
  • EDITORIAL: Tour boat safety needs beefing up to prevent repeat of Kazu I tragedy
  • VOX POPULI: In mystifying weather, we wait for second end of rainy season
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Guardian: 18 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: Russian shelling leaves six dead in eastern Ukraine, reports say
  • Daria Kasatkina comes out as gay and speaks out against Russian attitudes
  • ‘Not good enough’: Uvalde victims’ families react to report on police failures

  • Belgium sink Italy to reach women’s Euros quarter-finals for the first time
  • Tammy Beaumont ton leads England to ODI triumph over South Africa
  • Geraint Thomas plays waiting game with Tour de France on a knife-edge
  • West Virginia judge blocks enforcement of 150-year-old abortion ban
  • Judge broke promise to let Polanski go free in rape trial, sealed testimony says

  • Whiteness of England Women squad is stopping too many girls from dreaming | Anita Asante
  • Zinchenko due for Arsenal medical and £30m move after agreeing contract

  • Pop artist Claes Oldenburg, known for giant urban sculptures, dies aged 93
  • Anthony Fauci says he will retire by end of Joe Biden’s first term
  • Uvalde body camera video shows some police knew they needed to act quickly
  • The Guardian view on Italy’s political crisis: Draghi should stay for now | Editorial
  • Barcelona’s summer spree is not about building a team but selling a story | Jonathan Liew
  • Folly or art? Catalonian town buys labyrinthine Espai Corberó for €3m
  • First dormant black hole found outside the Milky Way
  • January 6 committee to receive deleted Secret Service texts, Democrat says
  • Russia’s Gazprom tells European buyers it cannot guarantee gas supplies
  • Empire of the drones: Terrence Howard takes us where no actor has gone before
  • ‘Heat apocalypse’ warning in western France as thousands flee wildfire
  • Climate-crisis anxiety denial is everywhere. But this week it’s impossible to ignore our worries | Zoe Willliams
  • Ranil Wickremesinghe: wily fox who’s been Sri Lanka’s PM six times
  • Cristiano Ronaldo could extend his stay at Manchester United, claims Ten Hag
  • £187m of Windsor family wealth hidden in secret royal wills
  • Emojis in the office: have they made your emails not suitable for work?
  • Stray review – press paws for adorable life as a post-apocalypse pussycat
  • Spanish customs seize €450,000 Picasso sketch at Ibiza airport
  • Energy fears weaken German taboos over nuclear power and speed limits
  • Push for post-Brexit trade deals may threaten UK pledges on deforestation
  • Trade across Irish border booms after Brexit amid energy growth
  • ‘I was traumatised at its demolition’ – Rachel Whiteread on making House
  • ‘Worth waiting for’: Jennifer Lopez and Ben Affleck (finally) marry in Las Vegas
  • This heatwave has eviscerated the idea that small changes can tackle extreme weather | George Monbiot
  • Rebel LIV tour and golf’s civil war overshadowed everything at the Open | Ewan Murray
  • Volodymyr Zelenskiy appoints new spy chief after Russian infiltration
  • Super Mario Brothers Karamazov: literature begins to take gaming seriously
  • How a conservative US network undermined Indigenous energy rights in Canada
  • The big idea: should we have a ‘truth law’?
  • People making a difference: the Grenfell volunteer cooking three-course lunches for 50 people every week
  • Germany faces calls to extend €9-a-month public transport ticket
  • How we met: ‘We were both grieving our spouses, and juggling children and the demands of work’
  • Weather tracker: UK regions expected to smash temperature records
  • Oil price rises after Joe Biden fails to secure Saudi output increase
  • I grew up where Liz Truss did, attended the same school. She’s not telling you the truth | Martin Pengelly
  • Next Tory leader must have clear plan on net zero, says Alok Sharma
  • Bewildered, bored, delighted? The Disney classics my kids loved – and the ones they couldn’t finish
  • What happened when I stopped hurrying – and discovered the joy of slowing down? | Joan Bakewell
  • ‘If the dustmen sing my songs, I feel honoured!’: Heather Small on 90s pop, racism and embracing her Britishness
  • ‘They have come to destroy us’: Ukrainians on the frontline in Donbas
  • Sri Lanka: acting president declares state of emergency ahead of MPs’ vote
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian
